name: Game Making@CV
description:
Game Making at Carmel Valley

Give your child the thrill of making their very first video game with block coding, a beginner friendly way to learn coding. Kids in grades 3 through 12 will work alongside a live instructor to design a main character, add movement, and create simple challenges--then watch their game come to life. It’s a fun, beginner-friendly way to spark creativity, build problem-solving skills, and get them excited about technology.

For programmers from 2nd to 6th grade.


Price: $375

Location: The League of Amazing Programmers
12625 High Bluff Dr #113, San Diego, CA 92130

Sign Up Below!
formatted_address: 12625 High Bluff Drive, San Diego, CA, 92130, United States

name: Competitive Robotics Summer Warm Up
description_short:
Students gain hands-on experience with robot design, build, and programming fundamentals aligned with competitive robotics. This summer session, we'll work together to learn what it takes to compete — meeting you where you are and pushing you further, whether you're picking up a wrench for the first time or refining your autonomous code.
What you'll gain:

  • Hands-on experience with robot design, build, and programming
  • Greater confidence heading into the season
  • Stronger teamwork and collaboration skills
  • A real competitive edge for the upcoming season
formatted_address: 1370 Wilbur Avenue, San Diego, CA, 92109, United States

name: Java Classes
description:
Learn to program in Java, one of the most important languages for commercial software development, and the language used in AP Computer Science. Many students explore our extensive curriculum and stay with our program through 12th grade.

  • Classes meet one day per week for 1.5 hours
  • Tuition is $280/month
  • Student–teacher ratio of 5:1
  • Taught by experienced programmers
  • Grades 5-12 only

    • Our weekly classes run every week throughout the year, so when you register, we will enroll you at the same class time every week (bulk enrollment). You can then cancel or reschedule individual classes online through the client portal.


    To register for weekly classes

    • Review the schedule below for a day and time slot. ( It's best to select the "Month" view. )
    • Create a client account , and add your students to the account
    • Enroll in a first class for the day and time you want, from the list of classes below.
    • To enroll in all classes of the same day and time, every week, you can:
      • Email Liz Groves at info@jointheleague.org ( Click to compose an email ) with the day and time you would like.
      • Call us at (858) 284-0481.
      • Your child's instructor can enroll your child at the beginning of class.
    For all details on enrollment, please see our Enrollment Guide and our Policies and Procedures.
description_short: Weekly Java classes taught in person at our Carmel Valley classroom.
formatted_address: 12625 High Bluff Drive, San Diego, CA, 92130, United States

description:
Learn how to use python, one of the hottest programming languages used today. Python is used in everything from machine learning to data science. Our online Python Classes are for kids & teens who are eager to learn programming. We work closely with all students to make coding fun and engaging. Many students explore our extensive curriculum and stay with our program through 12th grade.

name: Robot Garage
description_short: Students build and program robots and other electronic projects using microcontrollers, motors and sensors. Students learn basic electronics, soldering, mechanical construction, communication busses, motion control and other aspects of software driven electronic devices. This class is most suitable for students 7th grade and older, and students should have some programming experience.
formatted_address: 1370 Wilbur Avenue, San Diego, CA, 92109, United States

name: Intro to Robotics (Robot Riot)
description:
Build & Code: Micro:Bit Robotics

Take your coding skills to the next level by building and programming your own robot! In this exciting hands-on camp, students will use the Micro:Bit microcontroller to bring their creations to life. You'll learn how to program sensors, motors, and LEDs using Python, JavaScript, or block-based coding. Work with friends to design, build, and code robots that can move, interact, and respond to their environment. This camp is perfect for young engineers and programmers who want to explore the world of robotics in a fun and interactive way!

All equipment will be provided by The League of AMAZING Programmers. If you would like to keep your robot at the end of the course there will be an additional fee of $55.00 for the starter kit. As we progress through the course there may be optional add ons to the robot that can be purchased through amazon, those are optional and will be communicated if/when your child gets to that point.
formatted_address: 12625 High Bluff Drive, San Diego, CA, 92130, United States

name: Robotics Explorers@LT
description:
Robotics Explorers at After School Learning Tree

This week-long robotics camp introduces to the basics of robotics and coding through fun, hands-on activities. Campers will program robots to move, react, and solve challenges while building problem-solving, logic, and teamwork skills.

The camp focuses on creativity, confidence, and learning through play in a supportive environment that makes STEM exciting and approachable.


Price: $280

Location: 11525 Sorrento Valley Rd, STE A, San Diego, CA, 92121

name: League Labs
description_short:
LEAGUE Labs is our unpaid internship program for High School students to develop new LEAGUE classes. LEAGUE Labs Interns will create 90 minute classes, on a subject of their choice, and teach that class to other students in our Tech Club or Code Clinic programs. Interns will not only learn basic job skills, but can also explore a new topic, because the best way to really learn something is to try to teach it.

Interns in the program will gain important technical and soft skills with:

  • Tools like Github, Github Codespaces and Visual Studio Code
  • Programming
  • Product development and Human Centered Design
  • Marketing and Promotion ( you’ll need to beta test your class
